e.g. AI content creation, coffee brand collaborations, cross-border fashion

agentChat.threadReliability.panel.title: Thread Reliability
agentChat.threadReliability.panel.handof: This entry only covers runtime signals for the current thread. For external model analysis, prefer the Workbench handoff artifact main path: `analysis-brief.md / analysis-context.json`; Quick copy for AI is for temporary debugging, while Copy raw JSON (debug) is for programmatic analysis, archiving, or secondary processing.

agentChat.threadReliability.memoryBaseli: No significant change from the recent baseline for this turn.
agentChat.threadReliability.diagnostic.i: You are an AI task reliability analyst. Use the thread reliability data below to judge whether this task ran well. If it did not, identify the root cause and propose actionable fixes.
If Backend diagnostic aggregation conflicts with frontend signals, prioritize backend diagnostics as the higher-confidence fact source, then combine the rest of the context.
agentChat.threadReliability.diagnostic.f: 1. Overall, was this task good / average / poor? Start with the conclusion.
2. What directly caused the interruption, failure, wait, or drift?
3. Is this a model capability issue, prompt/planning issue, tool issue, context issue, human blocker, or product interaction issue? Multiple may apply; rank the primary cause.
4. Which issues are one-off and which are systemic defects?
5. If only 1-3 fixes can be prioritized, what should they be? Sort by priority.
6. If information is insufficient, name the missing logs, telemetry, or context.
